title: mentha × verticillata f. pluriglobula
intro: Mentha × verticillata f. pluriglobula, also known as Bowles' mint, is a cultivar of the peppermint plant, characterized by its compact growth and multiple globular leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and relieve headaches. The health benefits of Mentha × verticillata f. pluriglobula include reducing nausea and improving respiratory function, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Bowles' mint often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Pharmaceutical biology", mentha × verticillata f. pluriglobula benefits include antioxidant, anticholinesterase and antibacterial potential, similar to its closely related species Mentha aquatica, which was studied in the research.
